title: Unexpected Provisioning Package (Allow-List)
description: Template rule. Fires only for provisioning packages (PPKG) whose identity is NOT on your allow-list. Use this to suppress known-good vendor/recovery packages while still catching unexpected ones. This rule is OFF by default: enable it, set your allow-list regex, then disable the always-on rule ANALYZE-SEC-005 so only non-allow-listed packages alert.
explanation:
A provisioning package (PPKG) was detected whose identity does NOT match your configured allow-list.

- **Unexpected package:** `{{identity}}`

The `identity` value combines the package name, .ppkg file name, ownerType and packageId, so your allow-list regex can match an approved package by any of these. Packages that DO match your allow-list (e.g. OEM recovery packages) are intentionally ignored by this rule.

If this package should be allowed, add it to the allow-list regex (the 'value' of the 'ppkg_not_allowlisted' condition). If it is genuinely unexpected on a pure-Autopilot device, treat it as a potential manipulation and investigate how it was applied.

remediation.title: Triage the unexpected package
steps: Open the provisioning_package_scan event for the full inventory and the matching provisioning_package_detected event for this package's identity
Decide: is this a known-good package that should be allow-listed, or genuinely unexpected?
If known-good: extend the allow-list regex (e.g. add 'Acme Recovery' as 'Dell Recovery|HP Recovery|Acme Recovery')
If unexpected: investigate the application path (USB media, C:\Recovery\Customizations residue, custom image) and the device's chain of custody

remediation.title: Activate the allow-list workflow (2-step)
steps: Enable this rule and set the allow-list regex to match your approved vendor/recovery packages (case-insensitive)
Verify it behaves as expected on a known device
Disable the always-on rule ANALYZE-SEC-005 so only non-allow-listed packages alert going forward

templateVariables.name: allowed_packages
templateVariables.label: Allowed provisioning packages (regex)
templateVariables.description: Case-insensitive regex matched against each package's `identity` (name | fileName | ownerType | packageId). Packages matching this pattern are treated as approved and do NOT alert. Combine multiple with '|', e.g. 'Dell Recovery|HP Recovery|CONTOSO-BULK'.
